params.name: Hermes & Dionysus
params.description:
On Jan 13, 330 BC, Praxiteles carved Hermes and the Infant Dionysus.
Hermes & Dionysus is one of the few surviving original statues from ancient Greece, it depicts the divine messenger Hermes with the young god of wine Dionysus resting during his journey to visit the mountain spirits. Carved from marble, the sculpture shows Hermes in a peaceful posture, leaning against a tree stump and holding Dionysus in his left arm. However, the statue is missing Hermes' right arm, which is thought to hold …
